name: DS-01 increased urolithin A production, butyrate in low-baseline producers, and inflammation markers
summary: The synbiotic trial provides human metabolite-restoration target engagement but not aging-functional efficacy.
statement: In 32 healthy adults, a 91-day DS-01 synbiotic significantly increased urinary urolithin A production, made all synbiotic-arm participants urolithin A converters by day 91 versus 44.4% on placebo, increased fecal butyrate in low-baseline butyrate producers, and was associated with reduced systemic inflammation.
quantitative_note: The abstract reports 12-fold urinary urolithin A increase at day 7 and 49-fold increase at day 91, 100% versus 44.4% conversion capacity at day 91, and fecal butyrate increase in low-baseline producers.
caveats: This is metabolite target engagement, not a functional, disease, or aging outcome.
The intervention combines live microbes and polyphenol substrate, so it overlaps live-biotherapeutic and composition-modulation tracks.
The study is small, healthy-adult, and product-specific.
